title: 3. Bones
text:
First, let's start with the bones. Understanding just the biggest structures is enough for
cosmetic acupuncture. Most of muscles are attached to bones, and those attachments are
sometimes the name of the muscle, zygomaticus, for example, so you can understand the
physiology and kinetics of muscles easily by studying the anatomy of bones. Many other
explanations of cosmetic acupuncture are based on the anatomical structure of bones. The
most important bones are written in bold in following tables.
Cranial bones
Right lateral view8
Bones
Position
Frontal *1
Forehead, roof of eye orbit
Parietal *2
Roof and side walls of cranium
Temporal *2
Sides and base of the cranium
Occipital *1
Posterior part of the cranium
Ethmoid *1
Part of cranial base, eye orbits, nasal cavity
Sphenoid *1
Base of cranium, hold cranial bones

Facial bones
Bones
Position
Mandible *1
Lower jaw
Maxilla *2
Upper jaw, eye orbit, nasal cavity, hard palate
Zygomatic *2
Cheek, eye orbit
Nasal *2
Bridge of nose
Lacrimal *2
Medial wall of eye orbit
Vomer *1
Nasal septum
Palatine *2
Nasal cavity, eye orbit, hard palate
Inferior Nasal Concha *2
Nasal cavity
